descs: Ankle-deep in warm condensation. Something two floors down is humming a waltz.
Crates stacked by hands that measured differently than yours. The labels have worn to suggestion.
Pipes sweat along the walls, each one tagged in three dead shorthand systems.
Work lights still burn here on some forgotten schedule, faithful as dogs.
The floor is scarred where machinery was dragged away in a hurry.
A breeze moves through with nowhere obvious to come from.
Paint on the wall marks exits for a crew that never came back for their tools.
The hum of the city above is fainter here, like a radio between stations.
The walls stopped being built somewhere behind you and started being grown.
Cabling hangs in loops that look less installed than rooted.
The architecture breathes — slowly, politely, like it's trying not to wake you.
Bioluminescent veins in the wall brighten as you pass, then linger on you.
It is warmer here than any machine should need. The warmth feels attentive.
Every surface is faintly ridged, like the inside of something's palm.
You hear water, or something practicing the sound of water.
The corridor curves in a way the station's blueprints would deny.
This room is a memory of a street you've never walked, rendered lovingly and slightly wrong.
The geometry here answers to something other than the station's spin.
Lost signals drift near the ceiling like slow fish, murmuring their last sentences.
The floor reflects a version of you that moves half a beat late.
It's beautiful down here. You distrust how much you agree with that thought.
Someone — something — has arranged the debris into an almost-garden.
Music without a source, faint and patient, the kind played for one dancer.
The dark doesn't press in here. It makes room for you. That's worse.

exit_labels: past a curtain of dead cabling
through a hatch stenciled in dead shorthand
along a catwalk over dark water
through a seam in the grown wall
up a short ramp slick with condensation
through the blast door, jammed half-open
hidden_hints: A draft breathes from one wall — from somewhere that shouldn't exist.
The dust on the floor is scuffed toward a blank wall.
One panel rings hollow when your boot knocks it.
A hairline seam in the wall catches the light wrong.
battle_hints: Something is ticking softly beyond the {dir} door.
A slow, deliberate scrape repeats itself somewhere {dir} of here.
You catch a wet glimmer of movement through the {dir} exit.
Faint song — the luring kind — drifts from the {dir}.
